name: National Foundation For Credit
description_short: NFCC is a nonprofit credit counseling network offering free and low-cost financial guidance through 1,500+ certified counselors. They help consumers manage debt, budget, and avoid bankruptcy without charging fees.
description_long:
The National Foundation For Credit (NFCC) has operated as a trusted nonprofit since 1951, establishing itself as a foundational resource in consumer financial counseling. The organization operates a network of member agencies across the country staffed by NFCC-certified credit counselors who provide confidential financial guidance. According to their website, they have served 35 million people since 2006, positioning them as one of the largest nonprofit credit counseling networks in the United States.

NFCC offers comprehensive financial counseling services including credit card debt management, budgeting and financial planning, homeowner guidance, student loan debt counseling, and bankruptcy prevention guidance. Their primary service is helping consumers establish Debt Management Plans (DMPs), which they position as a safer, less costly alternative to other debt resolution methods. Services are available through multiple channels: phone counseling (800-388-2227), video chat, and in-person consultations with local member agencies. They also provide educational resources including online courses, FAQs, a financial glossary, and planning tools.

NFCC distinguishes itself through its nonprofit status, certified counselor network, and emphasis on affordability and transparency. The organization prominently features client testimonials and emphasizes that using their tools does not affect credit scores. Their membership structure allows them to connect consumers with local agencies, providing geographic accessibility. They offer services in both English and Spanish, addressing diverse consumer populations.

A key caveat is that NFCC's primary service—Debt Management Plans—works best for credit card debt specifically and requires consumers to commit to a structured repayment plan, which may take 3-7 years. While they market themselves as free or low-cost, individual member agencies may charge nominal fees. The website does not clearly disclose fee structures upfront, and effectiveness depends heavily on consumer discipline and the specific member agency's quality.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Established nonprofit since 1951 with 35 million people served since 2006, providing credibility and track record
1,500+ NFCC-certified credit counselors ensuring quality and standardized counseling practices
Multiple access channels: phone (800-388-2227), video chat, and in-person consultations with local member agencies
Services in both English and Spanish, making them accessible to diverse populations
Free or low-cost counseling model removes financial barriers for consumers seeking help
Debt Management Plans advertised to stop collection calls, lower interest rates, and consolidate payments into one monthly payment
Educational resources including online courses, FAQs, glossary, and budgeting tools available at no cost
cons: Debt Management Plans require multi-year commitment (typically 3-7 years) to repay debt, not suitable for urgent situations
Fee structure unclear on website—advertised as free/low-cost but individual member agencies may charge fees not transparently disclosed upfront
Limited to specific debt types (credit card, student loans, mortgages); may not address all consumer debt situations equally well
Relies on consumer discipline and follow-through with counselor recommendations; effectiveness varies by individual agency and counselor quality
No immediate debt reduction or settlement—focuses on structured repayment rather than negotiation or forgiveness
best_for: Consumers with credit card debt seeking structured, affordable repayment plans without credit score damage
Homeowners concerned about foreclosure or seeking mortgage counseling and homebuyer education
Individuals wanting to avoid bankruptcy and needing budget counseling and financial planning guidance
Low-to-moderate income consumers unable to afford paid debt relief services and seeking nonprofit support
diagnosis: NFCC is best for consumers with credit card debt or mortgage concerns who can commit to a multi-year structured repayment plan and want nonprofit, certified counselor support at low or no cost. The main caveat is that their Debt Management Plans are slower and more restrictive than debt settlement alternatives, making them unsuitable for those seeking quick debt reduction, and fee transparency could be clearer despite nonprofit status.
services: Credit card debt counseling and Debt Management Plan creation
Homeowner guidance and foreclosure prevention counseling
One-on-one financial reviews and personalized action plans
Online financial courses and educational resources
Budgeting tools and debt assessment calculators
Video chat and phone counseling sessions
Local member agency referrals and in-person consultations
Reverse mortgage counseling and homebuyer education programs
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2033 K St NW Suite 425, Washington, DC 20006
